D2 Vision L4 Analyze Reference

Double-Opponent Colour Fields

slide the centre and surround colours and watch a double-opponent cell fire hardest for a chromatic edge

A double-opponent cell compares colour across space: it likes one hue in the centre and the opposite hue in the surround, so it answers to chromatic borders, not flat washes.

Slide the centre and surround colours along the chosen opponent axis. The response peaks when the centre and surround carry opposite colours and collapses when both match — pick the red-green or blue-yellow channel to compare.
